Period comedy, starring Terry-Thomas, Sarah Miles, James Fox, Robert Morley, Benny Hill, Tony Hancock, Eric Sykes and Gert Frobe.(n)

programme.desc: The vets operate on Violet the koala to investigate her breathing problems, and the keepers wonder which will come off worst when a tree porcupine comes face to face with a skunk.(n)

programme.title: The Lakes with Paul Rose
programme.desc: The explorer digs out his rucksack and heads for Windermere in the Lake District. The National Park attracts 18 million visitors each year, and is now a World Heritage site. Here, Paul finds out why the early tourists thought the Lakes was one of the most dangerous spots in the country. He also plunges into the world of long-distance swimming and helps the staff who run the boats on the lake and meets a Holocaust survivor who was airlifted to Windermere in 1945 as part of a mercy mission involving 300 orphans after the Second World War.(n)

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland's Lochs
programme.desc: Paul Murton travels from the shores of Loch Hope to the lonely expanse of Sandwood Bay in the far north-west. Along the way he discovers wartime secrets in Loch Eriboll, crosses a subterranean loch and learns the art of drystone dyking from a master craftsman in Kinlochbervie.(n)

programme.title: Great British Menu

programme.title: Devs
programme.desc: Lily's investigation leads her to the man who knows Sergei's secrets, and stirs suspicion about her employer, Forest. But when he tries to throw her off the scent, she becomes more driven to find answers, and turns again to Jamie - although he is concerned how she will act upon whatever he finds. Mystery thriller, starring Sonoya Mizuno, Nick Offerman, Zach Grenier and Alison Pill.(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo

programme.title: Front Row Late
programme.desc: Host Mary Beard gets to grips with the latest broadcast technology as she aims to present the latest series of the arts review show from her study each week. And with cultural institutions around the country closed, the programme aims to bring content alive through interviews, discussions and exclusive performances from leading writers, artists, actors and musicians. The first show features an exclusive puppet production based on a short story by Edgar Allan Poe, created in confinement in Canada by leading author Margaret Atwood and her sister Ruth.(n)

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson examines the history of the Lancashire village Sunderland Point, which is cut off twice a day by the tide and has become a haven for wildlife. He reveals it was once a busy port that was instrumental in the slave trade, and at the forefront of the luxury goods business that transformed 18th-century Britain.(n)

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Six heat winners return for the quarter-final, fighting for a place in Knockout Week. Facing the Critic's Test, the week's best-performing amateurs take on an intimidating task - cooking to a brief set by food writer Jimi Famurewa, who asks for a dish inspired by flavours of the sweet shop. Presented by John Torode and Gregg Wallace.(n)

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Mediterranean Adventure
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore the culinary delights of Sardinia, starting out in Cagliari, where everyone is in high spirits for the feast of St Efisio. The bikers join in by cooking a local speciality, almond and orange cakes. They also meet the tuna fishermen of Carloforte and a chef who has a special way of serving up platters of the fish, are shown how to make sheep's black pudding by a group of shepherds and are served a stew by the 'King of Sardinia'.(n)

programme.desc: The Arderns reflect on the lessons learned through the series, and how corner shops can continue to thrive in the changing world of 21st-century business. The programme examines how shops have reinvented themselves to compete with mini-supermarkets, the rise of zero-waste shops, and how other businesses, like cafes, coffee shops and micro pubs, are filling the role of community hub.(n)

programme.desc: Four youngsters go boating while on holiday in the Lake District where they encounter two more children with a boat of their own, and a rivalry develops over a series of escapades. Family adventure, based on Arthur Ransome's classic novel, starring Virginia McKenna, Ronald Fraser, Sophie Neville and Simon West.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At Oxburgh Hall in Norfolk, David Harper is excited by a Polynesian war club, while Philip Serrell falls for a sewing machine dating back to 1875. Presenter Paul Martin uncovers the history of the Bedingfeld family, whose members have called Oxburgh home for 500 years - and managed to keep their heads on their shoulders.(n)

programme.title: My Life on a Plate
programme.desc: Brian Turner discusses culinary memories with Anneka Rice, revisiting her childhood in Surrey, and creates two dishes that take her back to that time.(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Mathematician Rachel Riley, singer and farmer JB Gill, radio presenter Katie Derham and comedian Richard Herring test their general knowledge skills, with an overall champion being crowned for the week.(n)

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: After weeks of speculation, Kate Humble and Ben Fogle reveal that the signs are that rare Amur tiger Yana is definitely pregnant. Anxious not to miss the historic birth of cubs - the first at the Park for nearly 20 years - keepers install special cameras in the Tiger House, which will also enable them to closely monitor Yana's heath at a safe distance. Plus, Jean Johansson discovers why coconuts are just the job for the dental health of Cape Porcupines.(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Secret France
programme.desc: The chef concludes his tour with trips to the Languedoc and Provence. In the border town of Uzes, Rick samples salt cod and marvels at a colourful array of French patisserie - selecting a seasonal fig tart to have with his morning coffee. He moves on to the harbour town of Cassis to try North African lamb chorba cooked by a local fisherman, while back in his country kitchen, he prepares a confit tomato and aubergine tarte tatin.(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: The green-fingered team guides team enthusiasts through seasonal highlights from across the country visiting stunning gardens, meeting the gardeners and finding out their secrets of success. Monty Don and faithful hounds Nigel and Nellie are at Longmeadow to bring inspiration as well as outline all the essential gardening jobs for this weekend. Co-presented by Joe Swift.(n)

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two highest scoring chefs from the North West go head to head to cook their six-course menus again. Not only do they need to impress food writer Matthew Fort, restaurateur Oliver Peyton and broadcaster and cook Andi Oliver, but also bestselling children's author and Laureate between 2011 and 2013 Julia Donaldson, who has sold over 65 million books worldwide and been translated into over 75 languages. Presented by Susan Calman.(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.sub-title: Pangolins - The World's Most Wanted Animal
programme.desc: Documentary examining attempts to save the most illegally trafficked animals in the world - a little-known scaly mammal called the pangolin. Based in Namibia, conservationist Maria Diekmann rescues and rehabilitates these local creatures. Cameras follow her to Vietnam, Thailand and China - into the heart of the crisis, where demand for pangolin products is greatest.(n)

programme.title: The Mash Report
programme.desc: A self-isolated Nish Kumar hosts Britain's most uncompromising topical satire, tackling all the troubling news from a world that's put its faith in Boris Johnson and Donald Trump. His similarly quarantined correspondents, include Ellie Taylor and Steve N Allen.(n)

programme.title: The Mini Mash Report
programme.desc: Nish Kumar and comedy correspondents Rachel Parris, Ellie Taylor and Steve N Allen present the satirical and surreal take on everything that happened in the week the programme first aired in May 2019.(n)

programme.desc: A small-town man is hailed as a hero after he shoots dead a burglar who had broken into his home. He begins to fear for his life when the thief's father turns up seeking revenge, which is only the first in a series of events that plunge his simple life into chaos. Crime thriller based on Joe R Lansdale's novel, starring Michael C Hall, Sam Shepard and Don Johnson.(n)

programme.title: Cops Like Us
programme.desc: Documentary following Staffordshire Police officers, who talk candidly about the frustrations of being on the beat in Stoke-on-Trent, one of the UK's most deprived cities. Filmed over six months, the series captures emergency response teams trying to combat a backlog of cases, as well as a rise in gang culture, hate crime and domestic violence. Interviews with the Chief Constable and his team also reveal how they manage their limited resources, and how they feel about the decisions they have to make.(n)

programme.title: Astronauts: Do You Have What It Takes?
programme.desc: The remaining candidates are put through more demanding tests by astronaut Chris Hadfield and his expert panel, with some pushed to their limits in a simulated emergency splashdown, where they have to escape from an underwater capsule. At one of the UK's biggest space-tech firms, they must successfully operate the Mars Rover, while a social event gives Chris, Kevin Fong and Iya Whiteley a further chance to scrutinise their characters.(n)

programme.title: Operation Wild
programme.desc: Broadcaster Clare Balding and vet Steve Leonard travel the world to watch vets perform innovative life-saving operations on a variety of wild animals. This first edition features a baby panda called Zhu Xia in south-west China in need of an MRI scan, and rescued gorilla Shufai in Cameroon, who was shot by poachers when he was a baby. Plus, a manta ray on the Japanese island of Okinawa is given an ultra-sound scan to determine if it's pregnant, and a four-ton elephant in Laos with a gunshot wound.(n)

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: As the competition continues, chefs Tom Barnes, Sam Buckley, Hrishikesh Desai and George Farrugia compete in the heats for the North West. programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs from the North West cook mains and desserts celebrating children's literature, and while one takes inspiration from JM Barrie and his own imaginary feast, the other two choose Beatrix Potter, leaving judge Michael O'Hare to decide which lamb dish is better.(n)

programme.title: The Â£100K House: Tricks of the Trade
programme.desc: Desperate to continue living in London, Adam bought one of the thinnest homes in the country. However, at six foot wide in places, the former alleyway was dark and barely habitable. Architect Katy Marks helps to transform his home with radical ideas, and Piers Taylor demonstrates how lighting, furniture and colours can make all the difference. Meanwhile, in Walsall, Hardeep and Rupinder have plenty of room for their family, but in all the wrong places. Piers Taylor designs them the ultimate social space, and encourages them to orientate their home around the garden.(n)

programme.title: World Snooker: Crucible Classics
programme.desc: Steve Davis v Tony Knowles. Hazel Irvine introduces action from the first-round match at the 1982 World Championship, staged at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield.(n)

programme.title: Talking Snooker
programme.desc: Alistair McGowan takes an affectionate look at the lives and careers of Alex Higgins, Jimmy White and Ronnie O'Sullivan, three of snooker's biggest stars. He examines the early TV appearances that captured each of them before they hit the big time, and charts the ups and downs of their respective careers, in an effort to capture a sense of why they were all known as the people's champion.(n)

programme.title: Porridge (1979)
programme.desc: Cellmates Fletcher and Godber are coerced into aiding an escape attempt organised by the sinister Grouty. However, the two jailbirds contrive to break back into prison before their absence is detected by the formidable Mr Mackay. Comedy based on the TV series, starring Ronnie Barker, Richard Beckinsale, Peter Vaughan, Fulton Mackay and Brian Wilde.(n)

programme.title: The Many Faces of
programme.sub-title: The Many Faces of Ronnie Barker
programme.desc: The story of a quiet, dedicated man who might have been a bank manager, but went on to become one of the nation's most popular comedy stars. Ben Elton, Michael Grade, David Renwick and Josephine Tewson are among friends and colleagues who remember his partnership with Ronnie Corbett in sketch show The Two Ronnies, as skinflint grocer Arkwright in Open All Hours and crafty prisoner Fletch in Porridge. There is also a look at his prolific writing career and his work as an actor in serious roles.(n)

programme.title: Great Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo visits the Thai capital of Bangkok, where he learns how the late 19th-century King Rama V built a grand palace and introduced railways to the country. Michael also learns that King Rama V's love of steam engines was inspired by Queen Victoria. Moving on, the presenter also confronts his fear of snakes to assist in a surgical operation on a venomous king cobra and steps into the ring to learn Muay Thai boxing.(n)

programme.title: The Sense of an Ending (2017)
programme.desc: Divorced and retired, aging Londoner and vintage camera shop owner Tony Webster whittles down the solitude of his isolated existence by keeping an affectionate relationship with his ex-wife, Margaret, and by accompanying his pregnant daughter, Susie, to antenatal courses. However, the unexpected arrival of an unsettling letter disrupts the fine balance of things in Tony's orderly life, reconnecting him with his first love from college, Veronica, and the nostalgic, yet clouded memories of a distant past. Drama, starring Jim Broadbent, Charlotte Rampling and Harriet Walter.(n)

programme.title: The Conversation (1974)
programme.desc: An obsessive, paranoid electronic surveillance expert - renowned within the profession as being the best - makes the unprofessional mistake of getting personally involved in a dangerous case on his San Francisco patch. Francis Ford Coppola's thriller, starring Gene Hackman, John Cazale, Robert Duvall, Allen Garfield and Harrison Ford.(n)

programme.title: BBC World News
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: How ESA's Solar Orbiter was built, how it will protect itself against the Sun's searing heat and how it will investigate the mysteries of the Sun. Presented by Chris Lintott and Pete Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: How to Make
programme.desc: Designer Zoe Laughlin sets out to redesign audio headphones, an intimate item that transport the wearer to another place. In search of inspiration she heads to Hull University to discover a new substance that can turn any surface into a speaker - from a wall to a table and even the bone of a skull. Zoe also looks at the device's history, from the stethoscope-style home contraptions of the 19th century and the game-changing 1980s Sony Walkman. But what will she come up with in her own design?(n)

programme.title: Mark Kermode's Secrets of Cinema
programme.desc: The critic examines techniques and conventions behind the heist movie, from The Asphalt Jungle and Oceans Eleven to The Italian Job and Baby Driver. He also reveals why this genre plays with peoples' sympathies more than any other.(n)

programme.title: Secrets of Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Part one of two. Jamie Bartlett investigates how technology companies are shaping the future of the world, and the potential negative consequences of the drive towards progress. He visits Hyderabad, where the utopian vision promoted by Uber has left many drivers on the brink of suicide, questions the impact of more jobs becoming automated, and meets a former executive who fears social breakdown and the collapse of capitalism.(n)

programme.title: From Ice to Fire: The Incredible Science of Temperature
programme.desc: Helen Czerski explores the science of heat, and how the ability to harness it has led to some of humanity's greatest innovations, from steam engines to plasmas.(n)

programme.title: The No 5 War
programme.desc: The story, both thrilling and dark, of the world's most famous perfume. In 1921, Coco Chanel's revolutionary perfume concept was as audacious as her outlandish designer clothing. At its launch it was an instant hit. From the 1920s to the 1940s the Number 5 brand was at the centre of a war between the celebrated designer and her entrepreneurial business partners, the Wertheimer Brothers. During the Second World War, with the help of her high-ranking Nazi lover, Coco Chanel attempted to oust her Jewish partners - who had fled German occupied France and were operating the business from New Jersey - to take control of the highly lucrative business.(n)

programme.title: England's Reformation: Three Books That Changed a Nation
programme.desc: To mark the 500th anniversary of the Reformation, Janina Ramirez tells the story of three books that defined this radical religious revolution in England. Tyndale's New Testament made the word of God accessible to the common man for the first time, The Book of Common Prayer established the Protestant religion, and Foxe's Book of Martyrs enshrined a hatred of Catholicism. Janina discovers how together, this trio of texts had a powerful cumulative effect.(n)

programme.title: Mark Kermode's Secrets of Cinema
programme.desc: The critic examines techniques and conventions behind the heist movie, from The Asphalt Jungle and Oceans Eleven to The Italian Job and Baby Driver. He also reveals why this genre plays with peoples' sympathies more than any other.(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Bruno Brookes and Jackie Brambles host the edition first shown on July 13, featuring performances by Waterfront, Bette Midler, De La Soul, Danny Wilson, Michael Jackson, Kirsty MacColl and Gloria Estefan.(n)

programme.title: How Pop Songs Work
programme.desc: Charles Hazlewood explores what makes a good pop single, breaking the songs down into six key areas - melody, song structure, lyrics, production arrangement and performance - before looking at successful songs in the genre. The conductor uses successful albums from the genre including John Lennon's Imagine and Amy Winehouse's Back to Black to examine these categories. Contributors include songwriter Guy Chambers, ABC singer Martin Fry and producers Steve Levine and Richard Niles.(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Gary Davies hosts the edition first shown on July 20, featuring performances by Sonia, London Boys, the Cult, Bobby Brown, Jive Bunny and the Mastermixers, Eartha Kitt and Bronski Beat, and Monie Love.(n)

programme.title: Sounds of the 70s 2
programme.sub-title: Sounds of the 70s - Rock 'n' Roll Revisited
programme.desc: The rock 'n' roll revivalism and 1950s retro leanings present in much of the decade's music, including glam and AOR. Featuring tracks by ELO, T.Rex, 10cc, Alvin Stardust and Mott the Hoople, culminating in Meat Loaf's 1978 Old Grey Whistle Test performance of Paradise by the Dashboard Light.(n)

programme.title: Elvis: The Rebirth of the King
programme.desc: When Elvis Presley began performing in Las Vegas in 1969, many believed that was the end of his career, especially after the excitement of his '68 Comeback Special. This documentary follows his time in the gambling capital and argues that it was a musical renaissance for the King, who reached his peak as both as a singer and performer - jumpsuits and all.(n)

programme.title: Sinatra: All or Nothing at All
programme.desc: Up-close and personal examination of the life, music and career of legendary entertainer Frank Sinatra, told in his own words from hours of archived interviews, along with commentary from those closest to him. Featuring previously unseen footage from home movies and concert performances, including his legendary 1971 'retirement concert' in Los Angeles, this tribute to the showman follows Sinatra's growth from roadhouse performer to global singing sensation.(n)

programme.title: Gregory Porter's Popular Voices
programme.desc: The soul and jazz performer explores the soft, intimate art of crooning. Born with the arrival of the microphone in the 1930s, crooning was initially about men seducing women and thrived through signature stars such as Bing Crosby, Nat King Cole and Frank Sinatra. However, far from disappearing with the advent of rock 'n' roll, the art of crooning gained a new existential edge and was transformed by the likes of Roy Orbison, David Bowie and even Lana Del Rey into a haunting and abiding strain of contemporary pop. Contributors include Iggy Pop, Joshua Homme, George Benson and Beck.(n)

programme.title: Music Moguls: Masters of Pop
programme.desc: Leading publicist Alan Edwards narrates this instalment looking at the PR plots that have helped shape some of the world's greatest artists. With the aid of rare archive footage, he explains how he and fellow exponents, such as Barbara Charone and Andy Saunders, cultivate new bands, manage crises and attempt to maintain the reputation of their long-standing clients. The programme highlights the campaigns behind superstars including Jimi Hendrix, Taylor Swift and David Bowie.(n)

programme.title: Elvis: The Rebirth of the King
programme.desc: When Elvis Presley began performing in Las Vegas in 1969, many believed that was the end of his career, especially after the excitement of his '68 Comeback Special. This documentary follows his time in the gambling capital and argues that it was a musical renaissance for the King, who reached his peak as both as a singer and performer - jumpsuits and all.(n)

programme.title: How Pop Songs Work

programme.title: The Private Life of...
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty reveals the hidden lives of farmyard animals, beginning with an investigation into the nature of cows. Visiting Devon and Northumberland, he discovers how the hierarchy of a herd is established, tests their intelligence by trying to teach three animals how to demand food using a bell, and discovers what could turn a placid creature into a potential killer.(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.sub-title: Coast Great Guides: Heart of England's South Coast
programme.desc: Neil Oliver and Tessa Dunlop present a guide to England's south coast from Brighton to Chesil, revealing what makes these two pebble beaches special. Neil also takes a trip on a flotilla of boats that includes a prototype yacht being prepared for the America's Cup, a vessel designed to help save the Solent oyster population and an offshore powerboat. Plus, a round-up of the best Coast stories from the past 10 years.(n)

programme.title: The Furchester Hotel
programme.desc: Funella has written the beginning to a new song to welcome guests into the Furchester Hotel. It's very very catchy. So catchy that it instantly gets stuck in Harvey P Dull's head and it's really getting on his nerves. Furgus suggests that he takes a walk in the garden to clear his head, but that doesn't work. Cookie Monster tries singing him a new song, the Cookie Cookie Yum Yum song but that doesn't work either. Elmo suggests that he tries counting, which works for a little while, but then the song comes back into his head! But the Furchester's can solve any problem and when it seems that Harvey can't take it anymore; Funella announces that she has finished the welcome song! Phoebe gets a monster idea: If they sing the song all the way to the end, then it might end in Harvey's head too. Everyone in the hotel sings the song and at the end Harvey is cured!(n)

programme.desc: Tim suggests baking some doggy biscuits as a way of cheering up Grandma's dog Mossy, who is not feeling very well.(n)

JB Gill discusses a watercress farm and shares a Spring poem. How daffodils are grown and picked is also revealed.(n)

programme.title: Where in the World?
programme.sub-title: Tyra, Rhys and Mario: Rhys learns to ice skate
programme.desc: Stories about the everyday lives of children living in eight different countries around the world. In this programme we visit three children who are all having fun. In Ghana, Tyra, who's five, is helping her mum cook tea along with little sister Tracy. In Canada, eight-year-old Rhys is learning how to ice skate - and taking a few tumbles along the way. And in Italy, seven-year-old Mario is escaping from the hot sun by playing a diving game in the swimming pool with his brother and sister.(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell is in her native Scotland looking round a flat in Dumfries, as well as visiting a plot of land in Birmingham, and Martin Roberts is at a house in Abergavenny.(n)

programme.title: Night Force
programme.desc: Documentary following the lives of the army of night workers. Here, the team at one of the country's biggest shopping malls installs a three-metre tall reindeer for Christmas shoppers. In Tynemouth the biggest fish catch of the year is landed with only hours to process the catch, while refuse collectors work hard to get Newcastle's streets clean and waste recycled before dawn breaks.(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp invites two teams to tour Detling Antique and Vintage Fair in Kent in search of items to take to auction in the hope of making the greater profit. They are helped on their endeavours by experts Caroline Hawley and Raj Bisram. Away from the contest, Natasha has a mystery item up her sleeve.(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The priest's priceless Blue Cross is targeted by infamous criminal Flambeau, so taking matters into his own hands Father Brown sets out to try to save the thief's soul, but risks falling into a trap. Mystery, starring Mark Williams.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin embarks on a house-hunting expedition in north Wales with a couple who have a Â£300,000 budget and a wish list including a craft room and community close by. Away from his quest, Jonnie cannot resist trying out Snowdonia's latest outdoor activity, catching a wave at the world's first artificial surf lagoon.(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Junk rejuvenator Sarah Moore rummages through the rubbish at Dunbar Recycling Centre in Scotland, where she's on the lookout for three unwanted items that she can save from the skip. Sarah calls on carpenter Bruce Faulseit to add a modern twist to a collection of bathroom bits, while blacksmith Kev Paxton tries to find a creative new use for a galvanised metal sheet. For Sarah's own project she has three dining chairs to update.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and Tim Medhurst explore the rolling Cotswold Hills in their 1970s off-roader. There are ancient artefacts, dry stone walls and lots and lots of sheep - but who will win out at the final auction in Devizes, Wiltshire?(n)

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: After an arduous eight-week journey, the search for the country's best amateur cook reaches its climax as the final three contenders push themselves to the limit for one last time before judges John Torode and Gregg Wallace. This is their very last chance to pull out all the stops, show everything they've learned along the way and demonstrate the kind of cook they have become. programme.title: Goodness Gracious Me
programme.desc: The first-ever episode of the Asian sketch show, first aired in 1998, starring Meera Syal, Sanjeev Bhaskar, Nina Wadia and Kulvinder Ghir. Highlights include life with the Coopers, nightclubbing with the Bhangramuffins, and why brown is the new black.(n)

programme.title: The Sapphires (2012)
programme.desc: An Irish music manager discovers four Aboriginal Australian singers in a remote mission in the 1960s, and plans to turn them into their country's answer to the Supremes. The naive girls face a challenging journey to stardom when they are sent to entertain American troops in the Vietnam War. Drama, starring Deborah Mailman and Chris O'Dowd.(n)

programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain creates dishes perfect for winding down at the weekend, including spinach shakshuka, a quick and easy squid risotto, a twist on Sunday lunch and an orange and coffee poke cake. Away from the contest, Charlie meets a record breaker with an amazing collection of Monopoly boards that cost Â£250,000.(n)

programme.title: FA Cup Football
programme.desc: A look back at the memorable 1990 semi-finals, as Liverpool faced Crystal Palace and Manchester United took on Oldham Athletic. Liverpool and United were expected to reach the final, but the underdogs in each tie helped to produce a day of enthralling drama. Presented by Guy Mowbray.(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore is in Surrey, checking out the cast-offs at Witley Recycling Centre, where she comes across a drum kit to repurpose.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a woman and her niece to search for a house in rural Gloucestershire, and also visits Owlpen Manor to find out how the Tudor property was rescued.(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Rich brothers David and Harry together with Arit Anderson travel to the West Midlands to help Richard and Darren create their very own slice of New York's 'High Line' garden in Solihull. The New York garden has famously turned an old disused, overground train line into a thriving garden that mixes contemporary paving, grass planting and historical, leftover train tracks. The couple want this garden to remind them of their honeymoon in the Big Apple.(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.sub-title: Pointless: The Good, the Bad & the Bloopers
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman presents a very special episode featuring a collection of bloopers, out-takes and unforgettable moments from the show over the past ten years, including classic nonsensical answers, exciting big wins, and a glimpse behind the curtain at parts of the show never seen before.(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: Scissor Sisters singer Ana Matronic, The Inbetweeners star Joe Thomas, former rugby union player Maggie Alphonsi and restaurant critic Jay Rayner answer questions on the specialist subjects of The Bionic Woman, George Orwell, Denise Lewis and the film musicals of Arthur Freed. Host John Humphrys then puts their general knowledge to the test before winner is announced.(n)

programme.title: Catchpoint
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts a family-friendly game show combining physical fun and an exciting question mechanic, as contestants have to think fast as they answer questions while trying to catch balls of various sizes dropped from the studio ceiling, with a variety of entertaining rounds and a cash prize at stake.(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Hospital drama with the staff of Holby's accident and emergency department as they deal with more sick and injured patients. Still feeling conflicted about his feelings, Dylan tries to avoid Faith as much as possible. Meanwhile, Rash goes against Connie's wishes when dealing with a challenging patient, and Ruby makes a life changing decision.(n)

programme.title: Gavin & Stacey
programme.desc: The couple announce their engagement, prompting the Shipmans to throw a party in Billericay. Even Bryn's decision to use his sat-nav for the journey fails to dampen their spirits, and everything goes swimmingly until the conversation turns to money - and the sweethearts drop a bombshell. Mathew Horne and Joanna Page star.(n)

programme.title: Outnumbered
programme.desc: Ben's school parents' evening takes an unexpected turn when he begins discussing muggers, dangerous chemicals and re-enactments of the Great Plague. Karen tries to decide which religion she would like to lead, while Sue still believes Jake is hiding something. Comedy, starring Daniel Roche and Ramona Marquez.(n)

programme.title: All Round to Mrs Brown's
programme.desc: Mrs Brown's Boys creator, writer and star Brendan O'Carroll dons Agnes's famous cardigan and curly wig once again as he throws open the doors of the larger-than-life Irish matriarch's home to host the Saturday night chat show. In the fifth episode of the series, Agnes and his comical family are joined on the show by the comedian and The Masked Singer host Joel Dommett, and morning television presenters Ruth Langsford and Eamonn Holmes. Plus, British country duo The Shires perform live on the show.(n)

programme.title: The Damned United (2009)
programme.desc: Fact-based drama chronicling Brian Clough's ill-fated 44-day tenure as boss of Leeds United in 1974, after Don Revie's departure for the job of England's manager. Following his acrimonious split from Derby County and a brief spell at Brighton & Hove Albion, Clough arrived at Elland Road on a wave of controversy, after previously being an outspoken critic of the club, and struggled to find allies among the playing staff. With Michael Sheen, Timothy Spall, Jim Broadbent and Colm Meaney.(n)

programme.desc: Mrs Brown's Boys creator, writer and star Brendan O'Carroll dons Agnes's famous cardigan and curly wig once again as he throws open the doors of the larger-than-life Irish matriarch's home to host the Saturday night chat show. In the fifth episode of the series, Agnes and his comical family are joined on the show by the comedian and The Masked Singer host Joel Dommett, and morning television presenters Ruth Langsford and Eamonn Holmes. Plus, British country duo The Shires perform live on the show.(n)

programme.title: The Damned United (2009)
programme.desc: Fact-based drama chronicling Brian Clough's ill-fated 44-day tenure as boss of Leeds United in 1974, after Don Revie's departure for the job of England's manager. Following his acrimonious split from Derby County and a brief spell at Brighton & Hove Albion, Clough arrived at Elland Road on a wave of controversy, after previously being an outspoken critic of the club, and struggled to find allies among the playing staff. With Michael Sheen, Timothy Spall, Jim Broadbent and Colm Meaney.(n)

programme.desc: First of two programmes in which Ross Kemp follows frontline medical staff in Milton Keynes Hospital Trust as they prepare for and face the coronavirus pandemic. He witnesses the sacrifices staff and their families are making in their battle against the disease. And he follows the journey of patients admitted into the hospital's respiratory ward with mild symptoms, through to the intensive care units, where those with severe Covid-19 fight for their lives.(n)

programme.title: Gordon, Gino and Fred: American Road Trip
programme.sub-title: Summer of Love (San Francisco and Napa Valley)
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay, Gino D'Acampo and Fred Sirieix head to San Francisco, the free-spirited capital of the United States. They start out by sampling the latest trends of using cannabis in cooking, which was legalised in California in 2018 - but as they enjoy a hippy afternoon tea, Gino overindulges and chaos ensues, especially when the next activity on the agenda is a session of goat yoga. Moving on, the trio dig for clams on the Pacific coast, take a hot-air ride to a vineyard and visit a factory in Chinatown where fortune cookies are made. It seems a competition is in order and so the travel companions see who can make the most cookies in 60 seconds.(n)

programme.title: Ainsley's Mediterranean Cookbook
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott's tour of Sardinia continues in the Nuoro region, where his guide Claudio takes him to meet his parents, who run a small bakery specialising in pane carasau, a thin crispy bread found all over the island. Claudio then takes the chef to his family's citrus farm to discover local fruit the pompia, a citrus so bitter it is never eaten raw but makes a fabulous liqueur. Moving up to the hills, Ainsley cooks a traditional sheep stew with a group of shepherds, visits one of the island's ancient archaeological sites and cooks up a Sardinian-style minestrone.(n)

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and the team heads to the Yorkshire Dales to surprise Jim Thompson, a terminally ill 65-year-old grandfather and fundraiser. Despite Jim's own diagnosis, his commitment to spreading awareness and raising money for prostate cancer charities are legendary in the area, including creating a 'Calendar Girls' style naked calendar. Inspired by his bravery and dedication, the makeover team decide to turn Jim's overgrown and dangerous plot into a classic cottage garden with a Yorkshire twist.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders

programme.desc: First of two programmes in which Ross Kemp follows frontline medical staff in Milton Keynes Hospital Trust as they prepare for and face the coronavirus pandemic. And he follows the journey of patients admitted into the hospital's respiratory ward with mild symptoms, through to the intensive care units, where those with severe Covid-19 fight for their lives.(n)

programme.title: Gordon, Gino and Fred: American Road Trip
programme.sub-title: Summer of Love (San Francisco and Napa Valley)
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay, Gino D'Acampo and Fred Sirieix head to San Francisco, the free-spirited capital of the United States. They start out by sampling the latest trends of using cannabis in cooking, which was legalised in California in 2018 - but as they enjoy a hippy afternoon tea, Gino overindulges and chaos ensues, especially when the next activity on the agenda is a session of goat yoga. Moving on, the trio dig for clams on the Pacific coast, take a hot-air ride to a vineyard and visit a factory in Chinatown where fortune cookies are made. It seems a competition is in order and so the travel companions see who can make the most cookies in 60 seconds.(n)

programme.title: Hatton Garden
programme.desc: With the surveillance teams hot on their tails, the gang members squabble among themselves over who should receive a cut of the takings. Will he cut Reader in on the deal? Timothy Spall, Kenneth Cranham and David Hayman star in the fact-based drama.(n)

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: A relaxed Will is installed as the new vicar, though is only just back at the vicarage when Geordie drops by requesting his help. Initially reluctant to be drawn in, Will is swayed when he learns that the matter concerns Adam, a profoundly deaf boy from a devout family, whose mother has recently died following a fall. Adam's father thinks his son was responsible and the truth needs uncovering to help the family heal. Will's own troubled past threatens to colour his view of the case - and Geordie realises his new friend has demons of his own. Drama, starring Robson Green and Tom Brittney.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot

programme.title: A Touch of Frost

The murder of a widow soon gives Frost a chance to prove his worth again, but the investigation only reveals an intriguing web of deceit, and it isn't until a forensic psychologist joins the team that they realise a serial killer is at work. With David Jason, Philip Jackson, Jeff Rawle and Danny Webb.(n)

Bizarrely, the key to the riddle seems to lie in a blackberry pie. David Suchet stars in the title role, with Philip Jackson, Hugh Fraser and Pauline Moran.(n)

programme.sub-title: Of All the Harbours in All the Towns
programme.desc: One of Joan's old flames comes back into her life and proceeds to woo her all over again, but Martin discovers the man in question has a life-threatening heart condition, presenting him with a difficult decision. The irascible doctor's problems do not stop there, as a schoolgirl develops a crush on him, leading to a confrontation with her irate father. Meanwhile, romance is also in the air for Al and Elaine. Comedy drama series, starring Martin Clunes, with a guest appearance by John Alderton.(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: An influx of holiday-makers to Portwenn annoys Martin, and his efforts to point out the dangers of sunbathing to a scantily clad woman prove unpopular. He is also the target of a practical joke in the pub after his fear of blood becomes common knowledge, and grows closer to Louisa when one of her pupils has a serious accident that lands him in hospital. Drama, starring Martin Clunes and Caroline Catz.(n)

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: Will is settling into the new routines of his life in Grantchester when an unexpected phone call reveals his father has got himself into some trouble, so he asks Geordie for his help. Will hasn't been home in a while, running from a past he wants to forget, but he cannot outrun his roots for ever and when one vicious beating leads to a grisly murder at a sprawling ancestral home, scandal threatens to engulf the Davenport family - suggesting life may never be the same for Will again. Jemma Redgrave and Nathaniel Parker guest star, alongside Robson Green and Tom Brittney.(n)

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: Reeling from his family tragedy, Will seeks distraction in Geordie's world where a curious case unfolds concerning the murder of a Teddy Boy at a dance hall. The dead boy's pockets reveal a library card from Esme's school, and even more disturbing - a picture of Esme herself. Geordie makes a beeline for the school and grills his frightened daughter. She pleads ignorance, but Will knows something is up when he spots her hiding a love letter. Away from her life at the vicarage, Mrs Chapman feels lost, while Cathy is left shaken by another encounter at work. Robson Green and Tom Brittney star.(n)

programme.desc: Asha is sickened to realise somebody posted her naked pictures and they have gone viral, while Leanne is furious to realise Steve is seriously considering taking legal advice. Johnny takes matters into his own hands and Ken's election campaign is on the up.(n)

programme.desc: British forces in colonial India find themselves in deep trouble when a manic local ruler discovers the soldiers' fearsome reputation is thoroughly undeserved - prompting open rebellion. Comedy, starring Sid James, Kenneth Williams, Charles Hawtrey, Roy Castle, Joan Sims, Bernard Bresslaw and Terry Scott.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A comic convention is held in Carver Valley, but the event gets off to a sinister start when the latest creation of a mysterious writer is stolen from the organiser's house and burned. Barnaby takes up the case, which turns murderous when a former supermodel is killed during the event. The only clue is an unusual issue of the missing comics, which seems to reveal the dark secrets of six local people - and provides the police with six likely suspects. Guest starring Bill Bailey and Jemma Redgrave.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.sub-title: ITV3's Most Wanted - Agatha Christie's Marple Viewers' Vote
programme.desc: The 'Most Wanted' episode of Agatha Christie's Marple, as voted by the public. Which classic of the genre will it be?(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When a pub landlady is crushed to death by a giant box during a magic show organised by famous illusionist Gideon Latimer, pathologist Kate discovers the cables had been deliberately sabotaged. Evangelising curate Andrew Maplin claims the tragedy is God's vengeance for the pagan traditions still held deep in Midsomer Oaks, but Barnaby and Nelson fear Gideon was the real target. Neil Dudgeon, Gwilym Lee and Tamzin Malleson star in the murder mystery, with Joe Absolom, Andrew-Lee Potts, Justin Salinger, Amanda Burton and Stephanie Leonidas.(n)

programme.title: Carry On Girls (1973)
programme.desc: The council of a seaside town is in dire need of a means to attract tourists to its resort, so announces a beauty contest. While the conniving organiser plots a series of publicity stunts, an outraged feminist group plans to sabotage the event. Comedy, starring Sid James, June Whitfield, Joan Sims, Barbara Windsor, Bernard Bresslaw and Peter Butterworth.(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jack spends a cold night under the Platts' caravan. Samir tells Jim he is nervous about giving his kidney to Tracy. Gail realises her relationship with Martin is worth saving.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The Platts enjoy being close together. Nick and Melanie skive off school. Billy takes Betty dancing at The Ritz and reveals some stunning news from the past.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Tempers flare while the bus trippers wait to be rescued. The Dingles unsuccessfully attempt to hitch a ride home. Zoe confides in Sarah about the prejudices she's received.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Betty insists on a refund from Eric for the French trip. Terry confides in Alan that he is missing Britt. Zoe rows with Emma and tells her to stop controlling her life.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An explosion in the woods is linked to the activities of a villain working out of a local quarry. Rachel suspects the owner of supplying dynamite to armed robbery gangs, so Joe goes undercover to infiltrate the workforce - but ends up in mortal danger when his cover is blown. Meanwhile, Peggy sees an opportunity to make money when rumours abound that a meteorite caused the blast. Guest starring Bill Ward and Graeme Garden.(n)

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: Roger joins forces with Mim to tackle a double challenge - the threat of a game being called off due to rain, and a challenge to his leadership from another member of the team. Cricketing comedy drama, starring Brenda Blethyn, Robert Daws, Timothy Spall and Josie Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: Roger's captaincy hangs in the balance as the votes are cast at the EGM, but Shirley provides an unexpected diversion. Cricketing comedy drama, starring Brenda Blethyn, Robert Daws, Timothy Spall and Josie Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby discovers there is a new man in Miss Jones's life and decides to declare his feelings for her - but his romantic gesture comes too late. Classic comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter, Frances de la Tour, Richard Beckinsale and Don Warrington.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Crime writer Ariadne Oliver (My Family's Zoe Wanamaker) and four known killers are among those who meet Poirot at a bridge evening arranged by wealthy adventurer Shaitana, who sits out the first game - only to be found later stabbed through the heart. As nobody has left or entered the victim's apartment during the night, the guests combine their skills to discover which of the party is responsible. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Lesley Manville and Alex Jennings.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Oscar suspects fishy goings-on after veteran music manager Stan Sparrow chooses to hold his wedding reception at the Aidensfield Arms. When Stan is found dead the next day, investigations reveal his demise may not have been through natural causes. Meanwhile, Geoff is baffled by a boy reporting a crime that has yet to happen, and Peggy's conscience starts to trouble her when she hears the lad has gone missing. Guest starring Sue Jenkins.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Nick is worried about Alice's first day at school. Barbara has some shocking news for Eric. Zoe arrives home to find Margaret Adlington confronting Emma.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Sam Dingle is convinced that Eric is dead. Terry gets ready to visit Britt. Zoe and Emma tease Linda about Biff. Dave stands Kim up in favour of a night in with Kathy.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ken worries about Denise and Dan's welfare. Tracy rows with Samir when he finds her drinking. Denise phones the salon to check all is well, but has no plans to return.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alf and Betty hand over their chains as their term of office ends. Ken heads to Macclesfield to find Denise. Tracy is troubled when Samir tells her he will be her donor.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Local boxer John Kinsella's successful world title bid stirs up hidden passions among the residents of Midsomer Morchard. As plans to stage a celebratory re-enactment of a Victorian prizefight shape up, jealousy, betrayal and blackmail take a ringside seat, while two suspicious deaths suggest Barnaby has let friendship cloud his judgement. Detective drama, guest starring Phil Daniels (Quadrophenia, EastEnders), with John Nettles and Jason Hughes.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Oscar suspects fishy goings-on after veteran music manager Stan Sparrow chooses to hold his wedding reception at the Aidensfield Arms. When Stan is found dead the next day, investigations reveal his demise may not have been through natural causes. Meanwhile, Geoff is baffled by a boy reporting a crime that has yet to happen, and Peggy's conscience starts to trouble her when she hears the lad has gone missing. Guest starring Sue Jenkins.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: English country squire Marcus Waverly receives a series of anonymous letters threatening to kidnap his son Johnnie, but the case is dismissed by Scotland Yard - prompting Poirot to open his own investigation. Drama, starring David Suchet and Geoffrey Bateman.(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The detective is faced with a difficult case when he investigates the discovery of a body in a well, and he also has to contend with a new boss, Chief Superintendent Moody, who is dubious as to Lewis' motivation for coming out of retirement. As investigations into the human remains in the well proceed, an avant-garde artist's body is also discovered and at first glance it looks like a tragic drugs overdose, but Hathaway and Lewis quickly recognise the tell tale signs of murder.(n)

programme.title: Hatton Garden
programme.desc: With the surveillance teams hot on their tails, the gang members squabble among themselves over who should receive a cut of the takings. Having performed such a professional raid, they are now behaving like amateurs, and with tensions running high, Perkins breaks his own golden rule by organising another meeting. Will he cut Reader in on the deal? Timothy Spall, Kenneth Cranham and David Hayman star in the fact-based drama.(n)

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: A relaxed Will is installed as the new vicar, though is only just back at the vicarage when Geordie drops by requesting his help. Initially reluctant to be drawn in, Will is swayed when he learns that the matter concerns Adam, a profoundly deaf boy from a devout family, whose mother has recently died following a fall. Adam's father thinks his son was responsible and the truth needs uncovering to help the family heal. Will's own troubled past threatens to colour his view of the case - and Geordie realises his new friend has demons of his own. Drama, starring Robson Green and Tom Brittney.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.sub-title: The Jewel Robbery at the Grand Metropolitan
programme.desc: On doctor's orders, the dapper Belgian sleuth visits Brighton for what he hopes will be a relaxing holiday, but it leads to one of the most absorbing cases he has ever faced - the disappearance of a cache of jewels. Mystery, starring David Suchet, with Hermione Norris, Philip Jackson and Sorcha Cusack.(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: The irascible copper finds his methods are called into question when a colleague is seriously injured during an attempt to thwart a suicide. The murder of a widow soon gives Frost a chance to prove his worth again, but the investigation only reveals an intriguing web of deceit, and it isn't until a forensic psychologist joins the team that they realise a serial killer is at work. With David Jason, Philip Jackson, Jeff Rawle and Danny Webb.(n)

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: As Brent Park Cricket Club prepare for the opening ceremony of their new pitch and pavilion, Mim arranges a big surprise for Rog - but disaster is waiting in the wings. Comedy drama, starring Brenda Blethyn, Robert Daws, Timothy Spall and Josie Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: The team celebrate a triumphant trip to Corfu. Back home, a Greek night is spiced up with a viewing of their holiday video.(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: A smooth-talking new tenant called Seymour, whose aristocratic veneer conceals the soul of an out-and-out crook, tries to con Rigsby out of his life savings. Classic comedy, with Leonard Rossiter and Henry McGee.(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: After receiving a chilling phone call, the detective and sidekick DC Presley stumble on an exotic animal smuggling ring at a farm - where it appears an old adversary of Frost has been eaten by a crocodile. Meanwhile, a couple return from holiday to find the naked corpse of a man in their bedroom, prompting the husband to assume his wife was having an affair. Starring David Jason.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Carol asks Joe for assistance when suspicions mount against her following the death of a pensioner who she frequently visited. Peggy is annoyed to find a pile of rubbish has been dumped in her backyard and Gina shares her concerns with Carol as she approaches the end of her pregnancy.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Biff is worried that he failed to impress Linda. Betty takes advantage of Eric's emotional state. Kim wants to know what Dave sees in Kathy.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Nellie fusses over Sam on his first proper day of work. Dave has a surprise in store for Kathy. Nellie and Zak's romantic meal out descends into chaos.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alma and Audrey spend a day shopping in Southport. Samir and Tracy have a counselling session with the consultant. Steve invites Fiona on a business trip to Ireland.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Emily thanks Samir for being selfless in giving Tracy his kidney. Ken is stunned when Denise tells him she wants to start afresh.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A planning dispute leads to murder after a stalwart in the Midsomer Conservation Society suspects her neighbours are involved in a money-making development scam. Political wrangling, burglaries and sexual liaisons abound in picturesque Great Pelfe as Barnaby and Jones investigate. Guest starring Hugo Speer (Bleak House), with John Nettles, Jason Hughes and Jane Wymark.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Carol asks Joe for assistance when suspicions mount against her following the death of a pensioner who she frequently visited. Peggy is annoyed to find a pile of rubbish has been dumped in her backyard and Gina shares her concerns with Carol as she approaches the end of her pregnancy.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective begins to suspect something is amiss when he visits one of his favoured haunts in London for dinner and discovers a fellow regular has dramatically changed his diet. Down in Brighton, elderly Anthony Gascoigne lies dying while his estranged twin brother Henry falls to his death - but nobody is sure whether the man was deliberately pushed. Bizarrely, the key to the riddle seems to lie in a blackberry pie. David Suchet stars in the title role, with Philip Jackson, Hugh Fraser and Pauline Moran.(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.sub-title: Of All the Harbours in All the Towns
programme.desc: One of Joan's old flames comes back into her life and proceeds to woo her all over again, but Martin discovers the man in question has a life-threatening heart condition, presenting him with a difficult decision. The irascible doctor's problems do not stop there, as a schoolgirl develops a crush on him, leading to a confrontation with her irate father. Meanwhile, romance is also in the air for Al and Elaine. Comedy drama series, starring Martin Clunes, with a guest appearance by John Alderton.(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: An influx of holiday-makers to Portwenn annoys Martin, and his efforts to point out the dangers of sunbathing to a scantily clad woman prove unpopular. He is also the target of a practical joke in the pub after his fear of blood becomes common knowledge, and grows closer to Louisa when one of her pupils has a serious accident that lands him in hospital. Drama, starring Martin Clunes and Caroline Catz.(n)

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: Will is settling into the new routines of his life in Grantchester when an unexpected phone call reveals his father has got himself into some trouble, so he asks Geordie for his help. Will hasn't been home in a while, running from a past he wants to forget, but he cannot outrun his roots for ever and when one vicious beating leads to a grisly murder at a sprawling ancestral home, scandal threatens to engulf the Davenport family - suggesting life may never be the same for Will again. Jemma Redgrave and Nathaniel Parker guest star, alongside Robson Green and Tom Brittney.(n)

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: Reeling from his family tragedy, Will seeks distraction in Geordie's world where a curious case unfolds concerning the murder of a Teddy Boy at a dance hall. The dead boy's pockets reveal a library card from Esme's school, and even more disturbing - a picture of Esme herself. Geordie makes a beeline for the school and grills his frightened daughter. She pleads ignorance, but Will knows something is up when he spots her hiding a love letter. Away from her life at the vicarage, Mrs Chapman feels lost, while Cathy is left shaken by another encounter at work. Robson Green and Tom Brittney star.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.sub-title: The Adventure of the Clapham Cook
programme.desc: The dapper Belgian detective, who usually only accepts cases of national importance, bends his rules for once and finds himself in run-down Clapham. He investigates the disappearance of a local cook and becomes intrigued by the contents of the woman's trunk. David Suchet stars, alongside Hugh Fraser and Brigit Forsyth.(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: After receiving a chilling phone call, the detective and sidekick DC Presley stumble on an exotic animal smuggling ring at a farm - where it appears an old adversary of Frost has been eaten by a crocodile. programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: Kevin and Maggie have an awkward conversation about their fertility problems, while Rog and Mim arrive to discuss the forthcoming marriage blessing. Cricketing comedy drama, starring Josie Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A woman's body is discovered in Captain Hastings' garage on Bonfire Night. It transpires that the deceased was due to marry an MP - and although the evidence suggests suicide, the Belgian detective suspects foul play. David Suchet, Hugh Fraser and Philip Jackson star.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length drama. While holidaying at the plush Bertram's Hotel in London, Miss Marple is presented with a fresh challenge to her deductive powers when the reading of a will leads to a double murder. Geraldine McEwan stars as the elderly spinster with a nose for a mystery, with guest appearances by Peter Davison, Mica Paris and Francesca Annis.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Dr Gaddas tells Steve he should take Oliver to A&E as a precaution, but a panicky Leanne arrives at the hospital, and is horrified to discover Steve left Oliver with Summer. Gemma finally accepts help for her post-natal depression, Ken's campaign gets off to an unfortunate start and Kelly encourages Asha to get back with Corey.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Amy's party soon gets out of hand when naked pictures of Asha are sent from Corey's phone to the â€˜party people' group chat and Corey accidentally smashes one of Tracy's new lamps. Leanne catches Steve red-handed, Johnny has an unwanted visitor and Ed and Aggie sit down to a romantic dinner.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Asha is sickened to realise somebody posted her naked pictures and they have gone viral, while Leanne is furious to realise Steve is seriously considering taking legal advice. Johnny takes matters into his own hands and Ken's election campaign is on the up.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Joyce Barnaby swerves off the road into a ditch, convinced she has hit someone, but nobody is found injured and Tom thinks she has imagined it. However, when a local historian discovers the body of a librarian in a nearby cemetery, Joyce blames herself for the man's death, leading her husband into a spooky investigation to find the real killer on the loose in the haunted village of March Magna. Guest starring Danny Webb (The Bill), with John Nettles, Jason Hughes and Jane Wymark.(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: The detective is approached by a former mentor from his college days, who asks Morse to ensure the safety of his wife. However, the assignment leaves him contemplating his future with the police force. Meanwhile, a murder in the world of fashion stirs up old gangland conflicts in the city. Detective drama series, starring Shaun Evans as the young Inspector Morse.(n)

programme.desc: The incomparable comedian performs a variety of sketches, with the assistance of guests Ronnie Barker, Thora Hird and Stubby Kaye.(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: A formerly famous theatre star is murdered, and the medical examiner resolves to track down the other half of her entertainment double act.(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: Theo suspects a murdered car thief who claimed to be coming into money is involved in an imminent heist - but has a week to find out where. Guest starring Ken Kercheval (Dallas).(n)

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Simon Templar becomes the target of a paid assassin and has to whittle down a list of potential avengers to find out who wants him killed. Time appears to not be on his side after a female journalist friend is abducted. Roger Moore stars, with Suzanne Lloyd.(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: The lure of easy money beckons once again as Terry and Arthur appreciate the commercial possibilities of cheap tobacco, but it is not long before the would-be smugglers are all at sea. Comedy, starring George Cole, Dennis Waterman and Ray Winstone.(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: The resources of CI5 are stretched when a mentally unbalanced former soldier starts killing hospital staff, believing them responsible for the death of his wife and for the brain damage suffered by his young daughter. Drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins.(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan and Carter track down those responsible for killing a driver and injuring a policeman while making off with vehicles carrying brandy on two separate occasions. Carter's new girlfriend, rookie cop Julie Kingdom, pursues a known fence and recovers the brandy. However, when Regan wants to take the credit, he and Carter come to blows. John Thaw and Dennis Waterman star.(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: The coroner tries to save a mortally wounded docker who is seriously injured and loses his arm, and discovers a group of doctors dedicated to even the most hopeless of cases.(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: A lawyer is murdered while working undercover investigating corruption in a major urban redevelopment project, and Theo uses some unexpected tactics to bring his killers to justice.(n)

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Simon Templar agrees to help a girl find her missing brother and becomes embroiled in a drug-smuggling operation. Roger Moore and Burt Kwouk star.(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur is repaid a debt in dodgy Â£20 notes, but by the time it comes to light they are counterfeit his creditor is heading on a flight to India. Once the Winchester is pinpointed as the source of the forged currency, Arthur also finds he has Chisholm breathing down his neck. Robbie Coltrane and Art Malik guest star, with George Cole and Patrick Malahide.(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle are asked to keep a surveillance on Cowley after evidence suggests he has stolen two canisters of a lethal nerve gas and begun negotiating with an unfriendly foreign power. The plot thickens when they trace their boss to a country mansion, where after they are caught he denies knowing them.(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A member of a group of convicts slips off the roof and dies during a robbery. The apparent leader of the gang appears to be having doubts about his life and Regan senses he is deliberately pointing clues in a desire to be caught during his next job. John Thaw and Dennis Waterman star.(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught.(n)

programme.title: Live and Let Die (1973)
programme.desc: James Bond's investigation into the murder of three British agents leads him to a connection between the dictator of a Caribbean island and a notorious Harlem drug kingpin - along with a plot to flood America with limitless supplies of free heroin. Spy adventure, with Roger Moore in his first appearance as 007, alongside Yaphet Kotto and Jane Seymour.(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.sub-title: A Number of Old Wives' Tales
programme.desc: Apparently successful entrepreneur Clive Cosgrove loses his 'confidence' after Arthur and Terry attend his wedding celebrations. Vintage comedy drama, guest starring Patrick Mower, with George Cole and Dennis Waterman.(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: CI5 is faced with a terrifying campaign of explosions and assassinations, apparently committed by an opposing operative that Cowley is convinced he killed several years earlier. programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A bad dream has Regan's girlfriend fearing for his safety, and her visions ring true when he gets involved in a case involving a stolen lorryload of cigars. programme.title: Giant Lobster Hunters
programme.desc: As the winter season enters its final few weeks, the price of lobsters rises above 100 dollars per kilo, and a bunch of new boats try to capitalise on the high price. programme.title: The Sting (1973)
programme.desc: Two conmen decide to swindle a mobster out of a fortune in revenge for the murder of a mutual friend. Oscar-winning Depression-era comedy drama, starring Robert Redford, Paul Newman, Robert Shaw, Charles Durning and Eileen Brennan.(n)

programme.title: Minder

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: The resources of CI5 are stretched when a mentally unbalanced former soldier starts killing hospital staff, believing them responsible for the death of his wife and for the brain damage suffered by his young daughter. Drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins.(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan and Carter track down those responsible for killing a driver and injuring a policeman while making off with vehicles carrying brandy on two separate occasions. John Thaw and Dennis Waterman star.(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: The coroner tries to save a mortally wounded docker who is seriously injured and loses his arm, and discovers a group of doctors dedicated to even the most hopeless of cases.(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: A lawyer is murdered while working undercover investigating corruption in a major urban redevelopment project, and Theo uses some unexpected tactics to bring his killers to justice.(n)

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Simon Templar agrees to help a girl find her missing brother and becomes embroiled in a drug-smuggling operation. Roger Moore and Burt Kwouk star.(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur is repaid a debt in dodgy Â£20 notes, but by the time it comes to light they are counterfeit his creditor is heading on a flight to India. Once the Winchester is pinpointed as the source of the forged currency, Arthur also finds he has Chisholm breathing down his neck. Robbie Coltrane and Art Malik guest star, with George Cole and Patrick Malahide.(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle are asked to keep a surveillance on Cowley after evidence suggests he has stolen two canisters of a lethal nerve gas and begun negotiating with an unfriendly foreign power. The plot thickens when they trace their boss to a country mansion, where after they are caught he denies knowing them.(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A member of a group of convicts slips off the roof and dies during a robbery. John Thaw and Dennis Waterman star.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Finance director Jane hosts the second night in Leeds. Her plan to grab the grand is to treat her guests to a night of fine dining, but she has her work cut out. Drama teacher Denver is already on the back foot with Jane, after taking offence to being called a 'goth' on the first night. Car painter Darren is concerned that Jane's menu of home-made ravioli, pork and panna cotta could win the week, but he's less impressed when it gets to the table. Painter and decorator Billy has noticed a chilling change among some of the other diners and Dionne is just glad her night is out the way so she can enjoy the rest of the week.(n)

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?

programme.title: The Interrogation of Tony Martin
programme.desc: Drama recreating the police interviews with the farmer who fired at a teenage burglar fleeing his home with a shotgun, the intruder being found dead the following day. The case divided public opinion, with Martin seen as a dangerous vigilante and as a vulnerable man trying to protect himself. The programme recreates the three days of interviews the police conducted with Martin following his arrest for murder, recreated verbatim from the original transcripts. Starring Steve Pemberton, Daniel Mays and Stuart Graham.(n)

programme.title: Digging Up Britain's Past
programme.desc: Helen Skelton and Alex Langlands, along with a team of archeologists, explore fascinating periods in British history through a current archaeological dig. In the first episode, Helen and Alex head for the island of Lindisfarne, where archaeologists are trying to locate the site of an eighth-century monastery. Among their finds is a graveyard and a number of skeletons, which may be those of both monks and pilgrims.(n)

programme.title: Britain's Greatest Bridges
programme.desc: Engineer Rob Bell sets out on a journey to discover how six of Britain's most stunning bridges were designed and built. Completed in 1890, the Forth Rail Bridge now carries around 200 trains every day. The last of the great Victorian engineering triumphs, it has grown to symbolise Scotland as much as Edinburgh Castle, bagpipes or kilts. But, as Rob learns, this incredible engineering achievement was born from the tragedy of the 1879 Tay Bridge disaster, and 73 deaths were connected with its construction and immediate aftermath.(n)

programme.title: The Wonderful World of Crafting
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley, Rosie Wolfenden and four passionate hobby crafters head to the Haddon Hall Craft Fair in Bakewell, Derbyshire. Brian loves turning old furniture into new pieces, so Rosie offers tips on how to photograph and present his pieces for sale. James and Bridget have an alpaca farm and make yarn by spinning. Bridget learns about natural dyes, while James rushes to get production running with his sock-making machine. Mum-of-two Tracey is a paper crafter who makes original pieces for friends and family, but she urgently needs advice on pricing.(n)

programme.title: Digging Up Britain's Past
programme.desc: Helen Skelton and Alex Langlands explore the history of Stonehenge, examining the stones that make up the structure, their acoustic properties, and how they were transported 120 miles from their origins in Salisbury Plain to form the circle. Helen also visits a village where builders were believed to have lived during the site's construction and meets scientists who have analysed the bodies buried beneath the monument, including the remains of a man who is believed to have come to Stonehenge all the way from Switzerland.(n)

programme.title: Walking Britain's Lost Railways
programme.desc: Rob Bell heads to Norfolk to follow a lost railway through some of the quietest - yet wealthiest - parts of the country. This railway was a curious latecomer when it was built in the 1880s and it threatened to rudely thrust the region into the industrial age. Rob explores an entire engineering town built from scratch, learns how Norfolk's agriculture was super-charged, and sees for himself where thousands of holidaying Midlanders arrived to sail the Norfolk Broads. The line put Norfolk on the frontline of the war so Rob takes to the skies to spot dozens of hastily constructed airfields.(n)

programme.title: Around the World by Train with Tony Robinson
programme.desc: The actor sets off from Seattle on an Amtrak Cascades train into Canada, where he is struck by the beauty of Vancouver and the warmth of the locals. He then takes a two-day rail journey aboard the Rocky Mountaineer. While the other passengers relax and enjoy the luxury service, Tony is put to work in its kitchen. Lastly, he travels via Alaska to the small town of Talkeetna. Boarding a float plane, Tony flies over snow-covered mountains to reach the base of the Alaskan glaciers, where he witnesses the impact of climate change up close.(n)

programme.title: Comedy Gold: Britain's Favourite Entertainers
programme.desc: Sue Perkins introduces a selection of her favourite comedy performances from stage and screen, including some rarely broadcast footage. Featuring clips of Victoria Wood, Graham Norton, Joan Rivers, Bruce Forsyth, Bob Monkhouse and Dame Edna Everage, as well as Muhammad Ali displaying his quick wit in chat show appearances.(n)

programme.title: Steptoe and Son Ride Again (1973)
programme.desc: Rag-and-bone man Harold invests his father Albert's life savings in buying a racing greyhound. However, the dog is in no fit state to compete, and after a disastrous debut, the hapless pair end up heavily in debt. Wily Harold soon has another plan to get the money they need - involving faking Albert's death. Comedy based on the TV series, starring Harry H Corbett, Wilfrid Brambell and Diana Dors.(n)

programme.title: The 21.co.uk Live Casino Show

programme.title: The Yorkshire Steam Railway: All Aboard
programme.desc: Under pressure to bring in extra revenue now that the somewhat challenging season is nearing its end, the North York Moors Railway team decides to put on a special 'curry train' night together with a massive bash for Halloween. Visitors and staff alike put in the effort to make it a night to remember, though proceedings are threatened when the lights in the carriages suddenly stop working. The Bishop of Wakefield comes to bless Eric Treacy, an engine that has been through a Â£500,000 overhaul taking over two years.(n)
